name: embedding_retrieval_public
description: A compact MTEB/BEIR-style public IR baseline for the embedding_retrieval suite (SUITE-EMB). Domain-neutral encyclopedic/technical query -> relevant-doc pairs used to measure a candidate embedding model's BASELINE retrieval capability. Contains no private data of any kind; all text is generic prose. The public-vs-domain delta against this baseline is the calibration signal.

docs.text: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants convert light energy into chemical energy stored as sugars, releasing oxygen as a byproduct.
Chlorophyll is the pigment in plant cells that absorbs light most strongly in the blue and red bands and reflects green light.
The water cycle describes the continuous movement of water through evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection across the planet.
A relational database stores data in tables of rows and columns and answers queries written in a structured query language.
An index is a data structure that speeds up row lookups in a database at the cost of extra storage and slower writes.
A hash table maps keys to values using a hash function and offers average constant-time insertion and lookup.
A compiler translates source written in a high-level programming language into machine code that a processor can execute directly.
An interpreter executes program instructions one at a time without first producing a separate machine-code file.
A neural network learns by adjusting connection weights to reduce the error between its predictions and labeled training examples.
Gradient descent is an optimization method that iteratively moves parameters in the direction that most reduces a loss function.

queries.text: How do plants turn sunlight into energy?
How does water move around the planet?
How is structured data stored and queried?
How can lookups in a table be made faster?
What is the difference between compiling and interpreting code?
How does a model learn from labeled examples?
